details.text: Derivative: الْجِنِّ (Al-Jinn) comes from the word جَنَّ – يَجُنُّ which means to cover, cover and conceal. Many other nouns come from it like the word جَنَّة (Jannah - A lush garden which is covered in vegetation and growth). Another word is جِنِين (Jineen – A foetus which is hidden inside its mother). الْجِنِّ (Al-Jinn) is a creature that is hidden from the eyes and ears, and whose movement is hidden from humanity. Also, one of the words for snake is جَانٌّ (Jaann) and is used for a large snake that terrifies you as though you saw a Jinn.
Derivative: عَجَبًا means something that is wondrous, unique and unparalleled. Something the like of which we have never heard before in terms of its eloquence and purity of speech.

Derivative: قِدَدًا comes from قَدَّ which means to rip, cut or carve. With طَرَائِقَ (paths) it means to split into different groups i.e., which are cut off from each other and follow different paths.
Derivative: نُعْجِزَ from أَعْجَزَ which means to render power or capability futile, incapacitate, cause to fail, undermine or escape from someone’s power or authority.
Derivative: بَخْسًا means a reduction or lessening in compensation. Something less in compensation that expected i.e., to be short changed.
Derivative: رَهَقًا – To have a greater burden piled on.

Derivative: الْقَاسِطُونَ – There is the verb قَسَطَ – يَقْسِطُ and the verb أَقْسَطَ – يُقْسِطُ and their meanings are opposites to each other. The first قَسَطَ means to act unjustly whilst أَقْسَطَ means to remove injustice or to act justly. الْقَاسِطُونَ comes from قَسَطَ and means the unjust referring to those who have abandoned the truth and followed falsehood.

Derivative: الطَّرِيقَةِ – طَرِيق means path and the addition of the Taa’ Al-Marbootah (ة) indicates to the singular occurrence of the word providing the meaning of the single path or only correct path i.e., Al-Islam.
Derivative: غَدَقًا means in abundant provision or supply. غَدَق is used to refer to the abundant supply water that opens the doors to a good and prosperous life.

Derivative: يُعْرِضْ – عَرَضَ means to present something. أَعْرَضَ – يُعْرِضُ means the opposite which is to ignore something that is presented, to avoid it, give it no attention and turn away from it. It implies that you recognise and acknowledge something and yet ignore and turn away from it.
Derivative: صَعَدًا means painfully arduous and difficult. It can also imply that the punishment keeps elevating and increasing.

Derivative: الْمَسَاجِدَ literally means in Arabic the places where Sajdah (prostration) takes place including the parts of the body that take part in Sajdah (prostration). The Sajdah (سَجْدَة - prostration) is also used to refer to the prayer and الْمَسَاجِدَ (Al-Masaajid) refers to the places which have been built to perform the prayer.
Derivative: لِبَدًا means to be piled up in masses, crammed up together.
Derivative: مُلْتَحَدً refers to a place that can be leaned or relied upon. It is a name for a refuge.
Derivative: يُظْهِرُ from أَظْهَرَ which means to make manifest and obvious. To make something ظَاهِر (Zhaahir - obvious, apparent, evident).
Derivative: أَحَاطَ means to encompass which means to have complete and perfect knowledge
Derivative: أَحْصَىٰ means to have a complete and precise count.
Derivative: شَطَطًا refers to speech that is excessive in lying and misguidance.
Derivative: رَهَقًا to be piled upon or covered in foolishness, misguidance and sin.
Derivative: شُهُبًا refers to shots of flaming fire, meteors or shooting stars.
Derivative: Lying in wait i.e., to strike them.

- The passive voice أُرِيدَ (is intended) which omits mentioning the doer is used when associated with شَرٌّ (evil and bad) whilst the active voice أَرَادَ (He intended) is used when associated to رَشَدًا (guidance). This indicates to the avoidance of the association of Allah directly with that which is evil or bad.
